title: Sequences and indexes deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Sequences and indexes; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Sequences and indexes: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A sequence of length four has a valid element at index four because length names the last position.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. For a zero-based sequence, the last valid index is length minus one; test empty, first, last, and one-past-last cases explicitly.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. For a zero-based sequence, the last valid index is length minus one; test empty, first, last, and one-past-last cases explicitly.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Sequences and indexes instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: For a zero-based sequence, the last valid index is length minus one; test empty, first, last, and one-past-last cases explicitly.
A sequence of length four has a valid element at index four because length names the last position.

answer.explanation: For a zero-based sequence, the last valid index is length minus one; test empty, first, last, and one-past-last cases explicitly.
